title: Unix Timestamp Converter
metaDescription: Convert Unix timestamps to human-readable dates and back, instantly in your browser. Handles both seconds and milliseconds epoch formats.
shortDescription: Convert between Unix epoch timestamps and human-readable dates.

guideSections.heading: Why the Same Epoch Number Can Mean Two Different Times
guideSections.body: Unix time is conventionally measured in seconds, but JavaScript's own Date object internally uses milliseconds — mixing the two up is one of the most common timestamp bugs. A 10-digit number is almost always seconds; a 13-digit number is almost always milliseconds, which is the heuristic this tool uses to guess the correct unit automatically.

guideSections.heading: Why Showing Both UTC and Local Time Matters
guideSections.body: A raw epoch value has no timezone of its own — it's a count of seconds since a fixed UTC reference point. Displaying only the local-time interpretation can make a timestamp look wrong if you're debugging a server log written in UTC, which is why this tool shows both side by side rather than picking one.
